claims.evidence_text: Feekes growth stage 2-3 is when early nitrogen applications should be applied to enhance tillering in thin stands. Fall nitrogen application of 20-30 lb/acre is sufficient for developing fall tillers. Spring tillering in thin stands can be encouraged by applying 30-50 lb/acre at dormancy break with the balance of spring topdress nitrogen applied at Feekes 5.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat nitrogen timing: early N at Feekes 2-3 (tillering), main spring topdress at Feekes 5 (green-up); cutoff at Feekes 6.

claims.evidence_text: Fall nitrogen application of 20-30 lb/acre is sufficient for developing fall tillers.
claims.claim_summary: Fall nitrogen of 20-30 lb/acre sufficient for developing wheat fall tillers.

claims.evidence_text: Spring tillering in thin stands can be encouraged by applying 30-50 lb/acre at dormancy break
claims.claim_summary: Spring nitrogen of 30-50 lb/acre at dormancy break encourages tillering in thin wheat stands.

claims.evidence_text: Feekes 5 will not occur prior to vernalization (chilling), which occurs during the winter. After vernalization, wheat plants begin to differentiate.
claims.claim_summary: Winter wheat requires vernalization (winter chilling) before stem elongation and head differentiation (Feekes 5).

claims.evidence_text: Kernels will have a doughy or mealy consistency at 11.2, and by 11.3, kernels will be hardened. Harvest can occur once kernels are ripe at Feekes 11.4.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat harvest occurs at Feekes 11.4 when kernels are hardened and ripe.
